title: Seedance 2.0: Advancing Video Generation for World Complexity
core_contribution: Seedance 2.0 is ByteDance's native multi-modal audio-video joint generation foundation model based on a dual-branch MMDiT architecture; supports 4 input modalities (text/image/video/audio) and 22 multi-modal tasks (20 supported, 7 exclusive), achieving #1 on Arena.AI T2V (Elo 1450) and I2V (Elo 1449) leaderboards while outperforming Sora 2 Pro / Veo 3.1 / Kling 3.0 / Wan 2.6 across every evaluated dimension.
summary: Seedance 2.0 is a release/evaluation report (not a technical paper) for ByteDance's new native multi-modal AV generation model deployed via Doubao / Jimeng / Volcano Engine. The paper inherits its dual-branch MMDiT architecture from Seedance 1.5 pro: video and audio each have a DiT branch, joined by a cross-modal joint module for tight AV synchronization, with text injected via cross-attention/AdaLN. Outputs are 4-15s native 480p/720p video plus binaural multi-track audio (background + ambient + dialogue). Reference-to-Video supports up to 3 video clips, 9 images, and 3 audio clips. The Seedance 2.0 Fast variant uses multi-stage distillation for low-latency. Evaluation uses SeedVideoBench 2.0 (30 motion sub-categories, 17 audio sub-categories, 22 multi-modal tasks) plus Arena.AI third-party blind testing. Seedance 2.0 ranks first on every dimension across T2V/I2V/R2V, with the largest gaps on audio (Audio Quality 3.67 vs runner-up 3.36), motion stability (Motion Quality 3.75 vs 3.10), and multi-modal task coverage (20/22 vs next best 13/22). Seven tasks (VFX/Creative reference + Continuation/Extension) are exclusive. The paper does NOT disclose architecture / training / data / compute details — those are inherited from the predecessor Seedance 1.5 pro paper.
key_findings: Only model leading on every dimension in T2V (6 dims), I2V (6 dims), and R2V (5 dims) simultaneously
Audio is the largest moat: T2V audio satisfaction rate (≥4 score) is 51-68% vs all competitors below 35%
Multi-modal task coverage: 20/22 R2V tasks supported (next best Vidu Q2 Pro at 13/22); 7 exclusive tasks (VFX/Creative ref + Continuation/Extension)
Arena.AI Elo 1450 (T2V) / 1449 (I2V) at 720p output beats 1080p competitors — perceptual quality independent of resolution
Motion Quality fine-grained: 29 out of 30 subcategories first; Editing Rhythm 4.21, Multi-Entity Match 4.43
Seedance 2.0 Fast variant via multi-stage distillation inherits 1.5 pro's >=10x acceleration recipe
Honest about weaknesses: video extension (1.93 vs Veo 3.1's 2.78), multi-subject consistency, complex editing, Chinese opera audio prompt-following
limitations: Zero architecture / training / data / compute disclosure — paper is a release statement, not a tech report
Only compared against commercial closed-source models (Sora 2, Veo 3.1, Kling, Wan 2.6, Vidu); ignores open-source SOTA (HunyuanVideo 1.5, Step-Video, Open-Sora)
Video extension task still trails Veo 3.1 by 0.85 points
Seedance 2.0 Fast variant has no quality-vs-latency curve disclosed
Most evaluation scores from internal SeedVideoBench 2.0; only Arena.AI Elo is third-party verification
No information on inference cost (GPU-seconds per generation), making cost-quality comparison impossible

infra_impact.algorithm: Validates GRPO family (DanceGRPO, RewardDance) on visual diffusion at production scale; multi-stage distillation (Hyper-SD/RayFlow/MeanFlow) is essential toolkit for low-latency video gen.
infra_impact.kernel: Dual-branch MMDiT with cross-modal joint attention requires kernels handling asymmetric cross-modal sequence lengths; long video + binaural audio joint denoising stresses attention kernels (SLA-style sparse-linear attention becomes more valuable).
infra_impact.framework: AV joint inference at 4-15s x 720p x binaural is a new state-management challenge; multi-modal reference inputs (3 video + 9 image + 3 audio) require new conditioning scheduling primitives.
infra_impact.llm: Counter-evidence to 'LLM-as-foundation' narrative: best-path for video generation remains dedicated MMDiT, not extending an LLM context window to all modalities.
infra_impact.agent: Raises ceiling for video-generation agents (script -> storyboard -> video -> editing); 22 R2V tasks expand the tool catalog available to creative agents.
infra_impact.code: Closed-source — only API access via Doubao / Jimeng / Volcano Engine; no modeling code, ckpt, or training script available.

open_questions: Cross-modal joint module implementation: token-level vs block-level vs shared attention?
Seedance 2.0 Fast NFE count and quality degradation curve
Total parameter count, training tokens/clips, GPU-hours
Will weights be open-sourced (historical answer for ByteDance: unlikely)?
